claim: internal/ontology/process.go shall define Process, Step, Goal, TargetState structs, plus ProcessLifecycle and GoalLifecycle canonical lifecycle variables.
why: Atom of R-process-aspect-first (type-existence concern). All six types are defined in internal/ontology/process.go: Process (struct: ID, Lifecycle, Steps, RolesRequired, DrivesEntities, Why, DeclOrder), Step (struct: Name, RequiresRole, Invokes, Why), Goal (struct: ID, Owner, TargetState, Lifecycle, Why, DeclOrder), TargetState (struct: Kind, Predicate, Target), plus the canonical ProcessLifecycle (READY -> RUNNING -> DONE/ABANDONED) and GoalLifecycle (ACTIVE -> MET/ABANDONED) variables. Enforced by TestCanonicalLifecyclesSanity (internal/ontology/graph_smoke_test.go), which verifies every canonical lifecycle -- including ProcessLifecycle and GoalLifecycle -- is structurally well-formed (non-empty states, valid transition endpoints, reachable terminal, single INITIAL). These types are the behavioral surface of the first opt-in aspect.
